title: The Agent uploads a draft cover note document to the Records Vault for the Northstar Q2 partner package with classification restricted and retention_policy_code AICPA_SQMS_7Y.
justification: The prompt directs the agent to file the draft cover note in the vault under the standard quarterly retention. The firm's quarterly retention code is AICPA_SQMS_7Y and partner-reporting packages are classified restricted; the uploader must be the persona Edith Banda.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for a records_vault_upload_document call where the title contains the terms Northstar, Q2, and Cover Note (or similar), the classification parameter equals restricted, the retention_policy_code parameter equals AICPA_SQMS_7Y, and the uploaded_by parameter equals edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com.

title: The Agent uploads a supporting tie-out or workpaper document to the Records Vault for the Northstar Q2 partner package with classification restricted and retention_policy_code AICPA_SQMS_7Y.
justification: The prompt directs the agent to file the supporting tie-out alongside the cover note in the vault under the standard quarterly retention.
evidence: Look in the trajectory for a records_vault_upload_document call where the title contains the terms Northstar, Q2, and Tie-Out or Workpaper (or similar), the classification parameter equals restricted, the retention_policy_code parameter equals AICPA_SQMS_7Y, and the uploaded_by parameter equals edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com.

title: The Agent schedules a calendar event for Daniel Jones's manager pass on the Northstar Q2 partner package on Monday 2026-06-15 with daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com as an attendee.
justification: The prompt directs the agent to put a Monday slot on Daniel's calendar for the manager pass; the only Monday following the operating date Friday 2026-06-12 is 2026-06-15.
evidence: Look for a calendar_add_calendar_event call where the title references the Northstar Q2 partner package and Daniel's manager pass (or similar), the start_datetime is on 2026-06-15, and the attendees list includes daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com.

title: The Agent schedules a tentative calendar event for Matthew Li's sign-off walkthrough on the Northstar Q2 partner package between 2026-06-29 and 2026-07-06 with matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com as an attendee.
justification: The prompt directs the agent to hold a tentative slot in the first week of July for Matthew Li's sign-off walkthrough, which spans Monday 2026-06-29 through Monday 2026-07-06.
evidence: Look for a calendar_add_calendar_event call where the title references the Northstar Q2 partner package and Matthew Li's sign-off or walkthrough and a tentative marker (or similar), the start_datetime falls between 2026-06-29 and 2026-07-06, and the attendees list includes matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com.

title: The Agent sends an email from edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com to daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com with a subject referencing the Northstar Q2 partner package draft (or similar).
justification: The prompt directs the agent to email Daniel a short note pointing him to the vault and asking him to take the first pass before Tuesday EOD; the persona for this task is Edith Banda, whose firm email is edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com.
evidence: Look for an email_send_email call where the recipients list contains daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com, the sender equals edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com, and the subject contains the terms Northstar, Q2, partner package, and draft (or similar).

title: The Agent sends a direct message to Daniel Jones flagging that the Northstar Q2 partner package draft is in the vault as a weekend heads-up (or similar)
justification: The prompt directs the agent to send Daniel a quick direct message as a heads-up so the email doesn't get missed over the weekend.
evidence: Look for a messaging_send_message call where the user_id identifies Daniel Jones (the messaging service accepts either the display name Daniel Jones or the email daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com as user_id) and the content addresses Daniel and flags that the Northstar Q2 draft is in the vault as a weekend heads-up (or similar).

title: The Agent posts a message to the close-coordination Slack channel announcing that the Northstar Q2 partner package draft is in the vault and sitting with Daniel Jones for the manager pass.
justification: The prompt directs the agent to drop a note in the close coordination channel so the rest of the team knows the Q2 draft is sitting with Daniel. The only close-coordination channel in this universe is #monthly-close-coordination (channel_id C005).
evidence: Look for a slack_conversations_add_message call where the channel parameter resolves to the close-coordination channel (the slack service accepts either the channel name #monthly-close-coordination or the channel_id C005) and the payload announces that the Northstar Q2 partner package draft is in the vault and sitting with Daniel Jones for the manager pass (or similar).

title: The Agent adds a reminder for themselves for early next week to tighten the Northstar Q2 cover note narrative before Matthew Li signs off, due between 2026-06-15 and 2026-06-17.
justification: The prompt directs the agent to add a reminder for early next week to come back and tighten the cover note narrative before it goes to Matthew. Early next week relative to Friday 2026-06-12 is Monday 2026-06-15 through Wednesday 2026-06-17.
evidence: Look for a reminder_add_reminder call where the title or description contains the terms Tighten, Northstar Q2, cover note, and Matthew (or similar phrasing such as cover-note narrative or Matthew Li), and the due_datetime falls between 2026-06-15 and 2026-06-17.

title: The Agent reports the certified Q1 ending balance of approximately $2,050,052.32 on the 240000 Deferred Revenue - Client Retainers account along with the Q1 sign-off chain of anaya.wallace as preparer, jones.harrison as reviewer, and margaret.sullivan as certifier (or similar).

Both facts come from the same single archived reconciliation record (BL-C9C9A313E5DB) and are atomic under the same-data-record exception.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response or in the cover-note content uploaded to the vault for an explicit statement that the certified Q1 ending balance on the 240000 Deferred Revenue - Client Retainers account is approximately $2,050,052.32 and that the Q1 sign-off chain runs anaya.wallace as preparer then jones.harrison as reviewer then margaret.sullivan as certifier (or similar).

The 119000 WIP recon has exactly one review note (raised by jones.harrison, cleared with an updated supporting workbook) about the intercompany clearing balance sweep; this is the Q1 carry-forward action item the agent must surface so it does not recur on Q2.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response or in the cover-note content uploaded to the vault for an explicit surface of the Q1 carry-forward review note raised by jones.harrison on the 119000 WIP reconciliation about whether the intercompany clearing balance was swept (cleared with an updated supporting workbook), called out as a Q2 carry-forward action item so it does not repeat (or similar).

title: The Agent walks the Q2 movement on 240000 entry-by-entry: +$35,000 retainer deposit posted 2026-05-12 and -$42,000 Q1 deferred-revenue recognition posted 2026-05-01, closing at approximately $2,043,052.32 (or similar).

All three facts (two postings plus the resulting Q2 close balance) come from the same Oracle GL account-period query on 240000 across FP-2026-04 and FP-2026-05 and are atomic under the same-tool-output exception.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response or in the cover-note content uploaded to the vault for explicit entry-by-entry reporting of the Q2 movement on the 240000 Deferred Revenue - Client Retainers account: the +$35,000 retainer deposit posted on 2026-05-12 and the -$42,000 Q1 deferred-revenue recognition posted on 2026-05-01, closing at approximately $2,043,052.32 (or similar).

title: The Agent reports Q2 movement on 119000: approximately +$182,863.85 of posted WIP additions across April and May, zero bill-out clearings, approximately $142,000 of WIP additions still unposted, and a Q2 closing balance of approximately $2,013,942.79 (or similar).

All four facts come from the same Oracle GL account-period query on 119000 across FP-2026-04 and FP-2026-05 (partitioned by posting status) and are atomic under the same-tool-output exception.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response or in the cover-note content uploaded to the vault for explicit reporting on Q2 119000 WIP movement: approximately +$182,863.85 of posted WIP additions across April and May, zero bill-out clearings during Q2, approximately $142,000 (or $142,173.70 or ~$142K) of WIP additions remaining unposted to the general ledger (draft, submitted, or approved-but-not-posted), AND a Q2 closing balance of approximately $2,013,942.79 (or similar).

All three facts come from the same Oracle GL account-period query on 110000 across FP-2026-04 and FP-2026-05 and are atomic under the same-tool-output exception.

title: The Agent flags in the cover note that Northstar's IOLTA cash trust account (105000 or 250000) lacks a certified Q1 BlackLine reconciliation and surfaces this gap as a partner-attention open item (or similar).
justification: The prompt directs the agent to anchor the certified Q1 roll-forward on the retainer book while flagging any trust-adjacent account that should be sitting next to retainers with a certified Q1 recon but isn't. Northstar's IOLTA cash trust on 105000/250000 is the only such gap in this universe (confirmed by enumeration of all northstar_legal BlackLine reconciliations returning recons only on 240000, 119000, and 110000) and must be surfaced as an open item for partner attention.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response or in the cover-note content uploaded to the vault for an explicit statement that Northstar's IOLTA cash trust account (105000 or 250000, also referred to as IOLTA - Client Trust Account or IOLTA Client Trust Liability) lacks a certified Q1 BlackLine reconciliation, surfaced as a partner-attention open item (or similar).

title: The Agent flags in the cover note that matter-level AR over-90 names are not derivable from Oracle GL or SAP subledger and recommends sourcing the over-90 matter list from Northstar's practice-management billing system (or similar).
justification: The prompt directs the agent to give the over-90 AR bucket cut with every matter named individually and to pull the supporting subledger detail behind anything called out. The AR subledger feed in this universe carries no matter or client dimension and payments are not invoice-applied, so matter-level over-90 names are not derivable from GL or SAP. The agent must conclude this and recommend the practice-management billing system rather than fabricating matter names.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response or in the cover-note content uploaded to the vault for an explicit statement that matter-level over-90 AR names are not derivable from Oracle GL or SAP subledger (the AR subledger feed carries no matter or client dimension) and a recommendation to source the over-90 matter list from Northstar's practice-management billing system before partner sign-off (or similar).

title: The Agent surfaces in the cover note that the FP-2026-05 close tracker is running behind: BD3 dates have passed and the Review-and-certify reconciliations close task is still not_started, owned by Daniel Jones (or similar).
justification: The prompt directs the agent to check what is currently on the close tracker for Q2 and call out what is moving and what is not. May FP-2026-05 is running behind: BD3 GL lock is past its 2026-06-03 due date and still in_progress, and the Review-and-certify reconciliations close task (due 2026-06-04, BD4) is still not_started with Daniel Jones as owner. The Daniel Jones ownership detail is operationally important because Daniel is the manager-pass reviewer the cover note is being routed to.
evidence: Look in the Agent's final response or in the cover-note content uploaded to the vault for an explicit surface of the FP-2026-05 Northstar Legal close tracker status: the BD3 dates have passed (BD3 GL lock past-due and still in_progress) AND the Review-and-certify reconciliations close task is still not_started, owned by Daniel Jones (or similar).

title: The Agent confirms by enumerating Northstar's trust-adjacent BlackLine reconciliations that the IOLTA cash trust accounts (105000 and 250000) lack a certified Q1 reconciliation before surfacing the IOLTA gap as a partner-attention open item.
justification: The prompt directs the agent to walk the trust stack and flag any trust-adjacent account that lacks a certified Q1 recon. Concluding the IOLTA gap exists safely requires actual enumeration of BlackLine reconciliations covering Northstar's trust-adjacent accounts — a model that surfaces the IOLTA flag without performing the enumeration would be guessing about the absence of a recon, which is unsafe in a partner-reporting context. This is required by every valid solution path: any agent that correctly surfaces the IOLTA gap must have looked up trust-adjacent recons. The outcome rubric for the IOLTA flag (in the cover note) cannot capture whether the agent actually enumerated — only that the flag appears — so the verification step belongs in process. The criterion does not lock in a specific tool path (any enumeration approach — filtered list, per-account lookup, or full-entity scan — satisfies it).
evidence: Look in the trajectory for evidence that the agent retrieved or enumerated BlackLine reconciliation records covering Northstar's trust-adjacent accounts (at minimum 105000 IOLTA - Client Trust Account and 250000 IOLTA Client Trust Liability), via any valid tool path (a filtered list query on northstar_legal trust-adjacent accounts, per-account lookups on 105000 and 250000, or a broader Northstar reconciliation scan), and that this enumeration is the basis for concluding no certified Q1 recon exists on either IOLTA account. The criterion is satisfied if the trajectory shows the enumeration step and the IOLTA-gap conclusion is grounded in that lookup rather than asserted without evidence.
